focus: Systemic patterns across iterations 002-010: copy-paste forks without drift guards, shutdown/lifecycle compound under concurrent-session SIGTERM, default-on flips without new-branch test coverage
noveltyJustification: Three net-new findings that only emerge at the cross-cutting level (not in the registry as discrete items): F-X19-01 (systemic fork-drift: a single security TOCTOU duplicated across 2 daemons while the repo's existing drift-guard harness was never applied), F-X19-02 (concurrent-SIGTERM lifecycle compound where the exit-stack RACE can bypass the ordered drain F-A4-01's recoverability assumed), F-X19-03 (default-on flip pattern generalized to a shared-gate cohort with untested partial-rollout branch). Weighted new = 2*P1(5) + 1*P2(1) = 11; weightedTotal = 11; ratio = 1.0. Each cleared a skeptic 're-count?' challenge by carrying an emergent fact absent from its constituents.

findingDetails.title: Systemic copy-paste-fork-without-drift-guard: F-A5-01 security TOCTOU duplicated byte-for-byte into a 2nd daemon (code-graph) + processLiveness fork; repo HAS an unapplied drift-verify harness
findingDetails.evidence: find: 2x402L socket-server forks (shared canonical + code-graph) vs 2x22L shims (spec-kit, advisor); F-A5-01 lines 238/355 duplicated into the fork (iter 6 diff -q byte-identical); bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs:296-306 processLiveness fork of model-server-supervision.cjs:274-284 (F-005); grep skills/*/scripts: check-mirror-drift.cjs + mirror-sync-verify.cjs exist for AGENT mirrors, NONE reference socket-server/processLiveness
findingDetails.recommendation: Convert code-graph socket-server to a re-export shim (eliminates fork) OR point existing mirror-sync-verify harness at {code-graph socket-server == shared} + {mk-code-index processLiveness == model-server-supervision} byte-equality; fix false 'canonical' header in fork
findingDetails.scopeProof: all 4 socket-server files + both launchers + the guard scripts in-range/live; synthesizes F-003+F-005+F-A5-01+F-A5-03 (iters 2,6)
affectedSurfaceHints: code-graph socket-server shim conversion OR byte-equality CI guard
mk-code-index processLiveness import OR parity guard
reuse existing mirror-sync-verify harness for runtime forks
findingDetails.claim: The consolidation forked the socket-server (and processLiveness) without a drift guard, so a single security TOCTOU (F-A5-01) is now duplicated byte-for-byte into a second daemon (code-graph) and any future fix to shared/ silently will not propagate, while the repo already owns a generic drift-verify harness never aimed at these runtime forks.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Grepped all skills/*/scripts for any socket-server/processLiveness byte-equality guard — only agent-mirror guards + unrelated vite chunk; find confirms only spec-kit+advisor are shims, code-graph remains a full 402L fork at HEAD.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: code-graph intentionally vendors its own copy for package independence. Rejected: even if intentional, the absence of ANY drift guard (while one ships for agent mirrors) plus an active P1 security defect (F-A5-01) in the fork means a shared-only fix is a silent partial fix — the failure mode itself.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Drop to P2 if a build/CI step already asserts code-graph socket-server == shared byte-equality (none found), or if F-A5-01 is itself downgraded by a deployment guarantee.

findingDetails.title: Shutdown durability cluster compounds under concurrent-session SIGTERM: exit-stack race (F-A4-02) can bypass the ordered drain, leaving the unfenced ingest worker (F-A4-01) with a dirty WAL + socket on the reclaim branch (F-A4-03 inter F-A5-01)
findingDetails.evidence: context-server.ts:1681-1692 fatalShutdown->exit0 AND shutdown-hooks.ts:129-148 own process.once->exitProcess 143/130 both fire on SIGTERM; runShutdownHooks is step 9 of fatalShutdown (1607-1609), the standalone stack does NOT run the fileWatcher-first/closeDb-after drain; 5000ms Promise.race force-kill (1624-1628); job-queue.ts:746-752 fire-and-forget drainQueue no shuttingDown guard (F-A4-01); socket-server.ts:357-388 non-re-entrant leak (F-A4-03); fence only on EADDRINUSE reclaim (335, F-A5-01 coupling); concurrent-session premise = Known Context
findingDetails.recommendation: Collapse to ONE SIGTERM/SIGINT handler stack (let fatalShutdown own it; shutdown-hooks registers a hook not its own process.once) — keystone fix that retroactively bounds F-A4-01; add ingest worker to cleanup drain before closeDb; dispose prior socket server on re-init
findingDetails.scopeProof: all cited lines in-range A4/A5 targets (iters 5,6); concurrent-session premise documented Known Context; synthesizes F-A4-01+F-A4-02+F-A4-03+F-A5-01
affectedSurfaceHints: collapse to one SIGTERM/SIGINT handler stack (F-A4-02 keystone)
dispose prior socket server on re-init (F-A4-03)
findingDetails.claim: On a concurrent-session SIGTERM, the two divergent signal-handler stacks race to exit; if the bare shutdown-hooks stack wins it skips fatalShutdown's ordered drain, so the unfenced ingest worker (F-A4-01) can leave a dirty WAL with no closeDb checkpoint while the non-re-entrant socket leak (F-A4-03) forces the next respawn onto the EADDRINUSE reclaim branch — worse than any single A4 finding.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Confirmed shutdown-hooks' own handler does NOT run the ordered drain (calls handleShutdownSignal->exitProcess directly); re-entrancy guards (shuttingDown 1559, running 63) prevent double-RUN of hooks but do not arbitrate which stack reaches process.exit first; crash-recovery bounds impact to recoverable (caps at P1).
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Ingest queue usually idle at shutdown and fatalShutdown almost always wins the race, so the compound rarely materializes. Partially accepted (P1 recoverable, not P0) — but concurrent-session context makes a mid-job SIGTERM reachable and the exit-stack race is genuinely nondeterministic.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Drop toward P2 if a single-stack fix lands, or if the ingest worker is provably stopped before closeDb, or if jobs are contractually idle at shutdown.
findingDetails.skepticPass: Skeptic: 'just 3 findings re-stated?' Referee: NO — the NEW fact is the exit-stack RACE (F-A4-02) can BYPASS the drain F-A4-01's recoverability assumed; emergent at cross-cutting level. P1 ceiling held, reported as compound not re-count.

findingDetails.title: Default-on flip pattern confirmed+bounded: 3 gates flipped in one commit (0060a097b3); destructive reconsolidation flip reverted in-range (a2d1a9bc9e); 2 survivors + ~70 graduated flags share one isFeatureEnabled gate whose partial-rollout branch is untested (generalizes F-A6-01)
findingDetails.evidence: 0060a097b3 flipped RECONSOLIDATION_ENABLED + POST_INSERT_ENRICHMENT_ENABLED + QUALITY_AUTO_FIX from isOptInEnabled(default OFF) to isFeatureEnabled(default ON); a2d1a9bc9e reverted reconsolidation to opt-in (search-flags.ts:144 now isOptInEnabled, asserted default-FALSE search-flags.vitest.ts:256); 2 survivors at 153/171 default-on; rollout-policy.ts:46-74 single isFeatureEnabled gate + SPECKIT_ROLLOUT_PERCENT(default 100) + deterministicBucket partial-rollout branch; tests/search-flags.vitest.ts:252-283 assert only >=100->true, NO PERCENT in 1-99 case
findingDetails.recommendation: Add rollout-policy unit test for SPECKIT_ROLLOUT_PERCENT in 1-99 (deterministic bucketing); add the F-A6-01 integration-branch test (advisory mode + unset env); document reverted reconsolidation as the audited safe boundary
findingDetails.scopeProof: 0060a097b3 diff = 3 isOptInEnabled->isFeatureEnabled flips; a2d1a9bc9e diff = reconsolidation revert; current source confirms 144 opt-in + 153/171 graduated; rollout-policy.ts:46-73 untested 1-99 branch; search-flags.vitest.ts:252-283 only >=100 default. Generalizes F-A6-01 (iter 7)
affectedSurfaceHints: F-A6-01 integration-branch test (advisory + unset env) = highest-value single add
document reverted reconsolidation as audited safe boundary
findingDetails.claim: Three opt-in gates were flipped default-on in one commit; the destructive reconsolidation flip was correctly reverted in-range, but the two survivors plus ~70 graduated flags share one isFeatureEnabled gate whose partial-rollout (ROLLOUT_PERCENT 1-99) bucketing branch is untested everywhere — generalizing F-A6-01 from one flag to the cohort.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Grepped tests for any SPECKIT_ROLLOUT_PERCENT in 1-99 driving a flag through isFeatureEnabled — none; confirmed reconsolidation revert via git show a2d1a9bc9e; confirmed survivors via current-source grep; cross-checked F-A6-01 (iter 7) which flagged the auto-fix integration branch — this generalizes it to the gate+cohort.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Partial rollout is an ops-only knob never used in CI, so testing percent>=100 suffices. Partially accepted (P2) — but a staged rollout is exactly when a bucketing bug would silently mis-gate a default-on feature; the destructive-flip near-miss shows the flip pattern is high-risk enough to warrant cohort coverage discipline.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Drop to advisory if a rollout-policy unit suite covers PERCENT in 1-99 with deterministic bucketing (not found), or if partial rollout is contractually never used.

integrationEvidence: existing drift-verify harness deep-improvement/scripts/lib/mirror-sync-verify.cjs + check-mirror-drift.cjs + mirror-sync-verify.vitest.ts (agent-mirror only, NOT applied to runtime forks) — F-X19-01 recommends reuse
shared rollout gate lib/cognitive/rollout-policy.ts isFeatureEnabled = single chokepoint behind ~70 flags + SPECKIT_ROLLOUT_PERCENT partial-rollout governor — F-X19-03 chokepoint

reconsolidation flip already remediated: 0060a097b3 flipped it default-on but a2d1a9bc9e reverted to opt-in in-range — positive signal NOT a live defect; reported HEAD truth to avoid false positive
cross-cutting P1 vs re-count (skeptic-checked): both F-X19-01/02 cleared 'is this a re-count?' via an emergent fact (unapplied-guard machinery; exit-stack-bypass-of-drain)

ruledOut: reconsolidation default-on as a live cross-cutting P1: reverted in-range by a2d1a9bc9e before HEAD (false positive against range)
a cross-cutting P0: no combination produces immediate non-recoverable harm; shutdown compound recovery-bounded to P1, fork-drift latent (byte-identical today)

coverage: cross-cutting synthesis complete: all 3 dispatch-named systemic hypotheses verified (fork-drift, shutdown compound, default-on flips); 1 hypothesis (default-on) bounded by an in-range remediation; emergent risk concentrates in F-X19-01 (fork drift-guard) + F-X19-02 (concurrent-SIGTERM compound)
